sprint: Selection Confidence Sprint — ThumbnailEngine Clobber Fix + Option B (Refined) Selection Action Strip
task: root-caused, via a long console-diagnostic collaboration, why a selected Scene Object's canvas outline/resize handles were confirmed to fire their real draw calls at exactly the right coordinates/colour yet never visibly appeared on the user's own real page: ThumbnailEngine.generate()'s restore-to-editor-canvas step called SlideRenderer.render(buildPayload(...)) directly, bypassing js/app.js's draw() -- the ONLY function that stamps selectedSceneElement/selectedTextElement/dragActiveId/showSafeArea onto the payload -- and since every drag-move handler deletes slide.thumbnail before calling draw() (forcing regeneration on the very next mouseup), this silently wiped all selection/drag chrome off the visible canvas after essentially every drag+release. Fixed by replacing the bare render call with window.redrawPreview(), the same real choke point every other 'something changed off to the side, please repaint correctly' case already uses. Confirmed via a real git-stash A/B: the exact reported symptom (0/8 gold-painted handle centers after a real drag+release) reproduces on the pre-fix code and is fully resolved (8/8) after it. In direct response to this investigation, product pivoted toward a more robust interaction model rather than relying solely on canvas-drawn feedback: reviewed 3 wireframe concepts, chose 'Option B, Refined' (chunky corner grips + a fixed action strip that never overlaps the slide) with one explicit hard constraint -- any new tool that opens can never sit over the centerpane canvas. Implemented both halves: HANDLE_RADIUS bumped 12->18 and _drawResizeHandles rewritten from thin gold circles to bigger rounded-square grips (getHandleRadius() already fed js/app.js's own hit-test slop, so the bigger clickable area came for free); a new js/selectionActionStrip.js module + CSS renders a small, always-reliable strip -- ownership status matching Object Strip's exact badge wording, a friendly icon+name, Edit/Deselect actions -- anchored as a sibling ABOVE .preview-wrapper in index.html (never positioned relative to the selected object), refreshed from PageRuntime.notify()'s own dispatch list alongside CardDesigner/ContextPanel/ObjectStrip/TravellerSaveNotice. Reordering deliberately excluded, per the established 'Object Strip is the ONE reorder control' precedent. Verified via a new 15-assertion Playwright suite (chunky-grip radius/shape, the strip's visibility/content/never-overlaps-canvas geometry, and -- the actual regression proof -- a real drag+release confirmed to still show 8/8 gold-painted handles afterward) plus full regression: existing text-resize-grid and commitments tests pass unchanged; several older scratch scripts predating the Traveller Gateway were reconfirmed via git stash to already hang identically on the unmodified baseline; one old script's own fixed near-corner drag offset now correctly engages the widened resize-handle hit area instead of a body move -- confirmed via a dedicated A/B check to be the exact, disclosed, intended consequence of the chunky-grip fix, not a regression (a genuine center-body drag still produces a clean move-only override). Both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2) pass unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's own entry for the full account.
